# src/change-detection

Provides git-based change detection and SHA-256 content hashing for incremental documentation updates. Uses simple-git for repository operations and Node.js crypto for file fingerprinting.

## Contents

**[detector.ts](./detector.ts)** — implements `isGitRepo`, `getCurrentCommit`, `getChangedFiles` (parses `git diff --name-status -M` with 50% rename threshold), `computeContentHash`, `computeContentHashFromString`

**[types.ts](./types.ts)** — defines `ChangeType` (`'added' | 'modified' | 'deleted' | 'renamed'`), `FileChange` (with optional `oldPath`), `ChangeDetectionResult`, `ChangeDetectionOptions` (with `includeUncommitted` flag)

**[index.ts](./index.ts)** — barrel export of detector functions and types

## Data Flow

1. `isGitRepo` validates project is git-initialized via `git.checkIsRepo()`
2. `getCurrentCommit` retrieves HEAD SHA via `git.revparse(['HEAD'])`
3. `getChangedFiles` runs `git diff --name-status -M baseCommit..HEAD`, parses status codes (A/M/D/R*), optionally includes uncommitted via `git.status()`, returns `ChangeDetectionResult` with `FileChange[]`
4. `computeContentHash` or `computeContentHashFromString` generate SHA-256 fingerprints for content validation

## Behavioral Contracts

**Git diff parsing format:** `STATUS\tFILE` or `STATUS\tOLD\tNEW` (for renames). Status codes: `A` (added), `M` (modified), `D` (deleted), `R<percent>` (renamed). Rename detection uses `-M` flag with 50% similarity threshold.
